channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Canada ready to buy more American products to appease Trump's tariff threat, ambassador says
item.description: Canada’s ambassador to Washington says Canada is prepared to buy more from the United States including its next fleet of submarines to appease President-elect Donald Trump's concerns about the U.S.-Canada trade deficit.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel and Hamas getting closer amid concerns terror group rearming in Gaza
item.description: Israel and Hamas are nearing a cease-fire deal amid escalating death toll; 5 IDF soldiers were killed in Gaza on Monday amid growing warnings that Hamas is rearming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: Brazil’s President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese And Diarlei Rodrigues, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 100 illegal miners have died while trapped in a South African mine for months, group says
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — At least 100 men who were mining illegally in an abandoned gold mine in South Africa have died of suspected starvation and dehydration after being trapped deep underground for months while police tried to force them out, a group representing the miners said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Former ambassador to Denmark: NATO would be compelled to respond to Greenland invasion
item.description: Rufus Gifford, a former U.S. ambassador to Denmark, said in a Sunday interview that the NATO alliance would be compelled to respond to any invasion or incursion into Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Watch live: Biden delivers foreign address at State Department
item.description: President Biden began his farewell tour around Washington this week with an address on Monday at the State Department to highlight his work on foreign policy during his presidency. Biden was expected to defend his foreign policy legacy, including reconnecting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Prominent diplomat and jurist who heads top UN court named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators.
item.dc:creator: By Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it, a Ukrainian official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: World's largest religious gathering begins in India, hundreds of millions of Hindus expected to attend
item.description: The Maha Kumbh Mela, or Hindu pitcher festival, begins Monday in the northern Indian city of Prayagraj. Officials expect at least 400 million attendees over the next 45 days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden and Netanyahu discuss the latest in the effort to reach an Israel-Hamas war ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Joe Biden spoke Sunday about efforts to reach a ceasefire and hostage release deal in the Israel-Hamas war, a sign of the intensifying push to reach a deal before Donald Trump’s inauguration next week.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU ministers will consider easing sanctions on Syria at a meeting later in January
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — European Union foreign ministers will meet in late January to discuss easing sanctions imposed on Syria, the bloc's foreign policy chief said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BARAA ANWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in Romania’s capital against annulled presidential race
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Tens of thousands protested in Romania’s capital on Sunday against a top court’s decision last month to annul the presidential election after an outsider candidate unexpectedly emerged as the frontrunner.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspended South Korean President Yoon to skip first day of impeachment trial over safety concerns
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol will not attend the first day of his impeachment trial this week, according to his lawyer, who cited concerns for the suspended president’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, told the pope Saturday he was a recipient of the medal.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9
